message:
Refine V1 spec and architecture documentation

Restructure and clarify the comprehensive FrankenFS V1 specification
document (~225 lines changed) with improved section organization and
technical detail. Update proposed architecture document with refined
component boundaries.

- COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md: reorganized spec sections,
  tightened language, added missing edge case descriptions
- PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md: minor architecture clarifications
- .beads/issues.jsonl: issue tracker sync

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-h6nz.7.6","title":"Standardize operator-facing error taxonomy and remediation hints across CLI/TUI/evidence","description":"## Objective
Standardize user-facing error taxonomy and remediation guidance so operators get clear, consistent, actionable recovery paths across CLI, TUI, and evidence outputs.

## Scope
1. Define canonical error classes (configuration, compatibility boundary, replay, repair, pressure/degradation, I/O/corruption, unsupported operation).
2. Map each class to stable machine-readable codes and concise human-readable remediation guidance.
3. Ensure CLI/TUI/evidence surfaces present aligned wording, code, and next-action hints.
4. Add runbook cross-links from error codes to exact remediation procedures.

## User Value
- Faster incident triage for non-authors.
- Lower risk of incorrect recovery actions.
- Better automation ergonomics via stable error codes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Error-class contract is documented and referenced by operator docs.
- At least 10 representative failure scenarios emit stable codes and actionable hints.
- Surface-consistency tests fail on wording/code drift.

## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:06:49.499726130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:06:53.825687949Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_operator_experience","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.7.6","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:06:53.498710755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.7.6","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:06:53.663266787Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.7.6","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:06:53.825625242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.7.6","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:06:49.499726130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-h6nz.8","title":"Program Gate: end-to-end V1.1 acceptance (unit + e2e + logging + user-facing validation)","description":"## Objective
Final program-level acceptance gate for the post-parity roadmap.

## Required Validation Bundle
1. Workspace-wide unit/integration pass with targeted subsystem reruns.
2. Cross-subsystem E2E script pack proving mount/mutation/replay/repair/observability behavior under representative scenarios.
3. Structured-log and evidence audit proving critical user journeys and failure modes are traceable.
4. User-facing validation: CLI ergonomics, error messaging clarity, and runbook operability checks.

## Required Outputs
- Consolidated validation report (commands, outcomes, artifacts, known residual risks).
- Explicit release recommendation: proceed / hold, with rationale.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All child verification gates complete with evidence.
- No unresolved critical-severity correctness gaps.
- Remaining non-critical gaps are explicitly tracked as follow-on beads.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:59:26.315348216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:33.042926913Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_program_gate","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:59:26.315348216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:59:26.315348216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:59:26.315348216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:59:26.315348216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:59:26.315348216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:59:26.315348216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.6.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:59:26.315348216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:59:26.315348216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.8","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:33.042873192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":346,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Mandatory closure checklist (verification):
- Comprehensive unit tests updated (happy path + edge + error).
- Deterministic E2E scripts updated with scenario IDs and machine-parseable pass/fail output.
- Structured logging added/validated at critical transitions and failure boundaries; key log markers asserted in tests.
- Gate evidence captured via rch-offloaded checks/tests and attached in closure comment.","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:00:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-h6nz.9","title":"EPIC: Cross-Epic Verification Orchestration and Log Contract","description":"# EPIC: Cross-Epic Verification Orchestration and Log Contract

## Problem Statement
Current verification expectations are strong but can drift across epics without a shared scenario catalog, log-field contract, and artifact format.

## Why This Matters For Users
- Incident diagnosis is faster when evidence and logs are consistent across subsystems.
- Release confidence improves when E2E scripts and outputs are comparable across tracks.
- Automation is safer with stable machine-readable pass/fail and artifact schemas.

## Scope
1. Shared deterministic E2E scenario catalog and ID conventions.
2. Shared structured logging field contract for verification scenarios.
3. Shared artifact manifest + retention/redaction policy.
4. Shared verification runner conventions and quality checks.

## Done Criteria
- Verification gates across all epics consume the shared contract.
- Drift detection exists for scenario IDs/log fields/artifact schema.
- Operator and CI workflows have one canonical verification contract.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:29.535727968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:10:10.110144393Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_verification_contract"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:29.535727968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-h6nz.9.1","title":"Define cross-epic deterministic E2E scenario catalog and identifier strategy","description":"## Objective
Define a canonical deterministic E2E scenario catalog used by all hardening tracks.

## Scope
1. Define scenario taxonomy (happy/edge/error/corruption/recovery/degradation/unsupported-op).
2. Define stable `scenario_id` format and uniqueness rules.
3. Define required setup/teardown determinism constraints.
4. Define minimum scenario set each epic gate must cover.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scenario catalog is complete for all existing verification gates.
- ID conventions are stable and machine-validated.
- Missing-scenario detection is automated in CI tooling.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:29.695886321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:29.695886321Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_verification_contract","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:29.695886321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-h6nz.9.2","title":"Define structured logging schema and correlation contracts for tests/evidence","description":"## Objective
Define canonical structured logging fields and correlation rules required for verification and operations.

## Scope
1. Required log fields (`operation_id`, `scenario_id`, subsystem, outcome, error_class, latency buckets).
2. Severity mapping and failure classification taxonomy.
3. Cross-surface consistency requirements (CLI/TUI/evidence/log stream).
4. Drift checks for missing/renamed critical fields.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Logging contract is versioned and referenced by all verification gates.
- Contract includes field semantics and examples for success + failure flows.
- Contract violations are automatically detectable in tests.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:29.858771713Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:29.858771713Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_verification_contract","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:29.858771713Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","title":"Define artifact manifest schema, retention, and redaction policy for verification outputs","description":"## Objective
Define a shared verification artifact manifest and retention/redaction policy for reproducible audits.

## Scope
1. Artifact manifest schema (scenario metadata, command set, checksums, outputs, logs, environment fingerprint).
2. Retention and pruning rules for CI/nightly/local runs.
3. Redaction policy for sensitive data while preserving debug value.
4. Validation tooling for manifest schema conformance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All gate outputs can be represented in the manifest schema.
- Manifest schema validation exists and is tested.
- Retention/redaction rules are explicit and enforceable.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.023599244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.335568911Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_verification_contract","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.023599244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.184645771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.335522785Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","title":"Implement verification runner conventions and machine-parseable result format","description":"## Objective
Implement shared runner conventions for deterministic E2E execution and machine-parseable result publication.

## Scope
1. Standard command envelope and environment capture for E2E invocations.
2. Stable pass/fail JSON result schema with scenario-level details.
3. Exit-code and retry semantics for CI/nightly modes.
4. Conformance checks ensuring per-epic scripts adhere to runner contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runner conventions are adopted by all verification gates.
- Machine-parseable result format is validated in automated tests.
- Inconsistent script behavior is detected and reported with actionable output.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.493066058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.079511306Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_verification_contract","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.493066058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.645464252Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:30.885423555Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.9.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:09:31.079471552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -1710,143 +1710,140 @@ Phase A MVCC (`MvccStore`) is currently in-memory only. This section defines the
 
 #### 5.9.2 High-Level Model
 
-The durable MVCC store is an **append-only log** stored separately from the underlying filesystem image (an “overlay”):
+The durable MVCC store is an **append-only commit log** in a byte-addressed durable region:
 
 - Reads consult the in-memory MVCC index first; when no version exists, fall back to the base `BlockDevice`.
-- Commits append new block versions to the overlay log and then append a **commit marker** that makes the commit durable/visible on replay.
+- Commits are serialized into a single checksummed `COMMIT` record containing all block writes for that transaction.
+- Replay applies only valid, monotonically increasing commit records.
 
-This is WAL-like: `VERSION*` records then a `COMMIT` record. A commit exists iff its `COMMIT` record is present and valid.
+The byte-region location is intentionally decoupled from the wire format:
+
+- It MAY be a sidecar WAL file, a hidden inode, or another `ByteDevice` implementation.
+- The record format and replay rules below are normative regardless of placement.
 
 #### 5.9.3 Storage Interface (informative)
 
-The durable store is byte-addressed and uses the canonical `ByteDevice` (Section 9.1).
+The durable store is byte-addressed and uses the canonical `ByteDevice` (Section 9.1). A reference implementation shape:
 
 ```rust
-/// Durable append-only store for MVCC block versions.
-///
-/// This trait is informative (planned) until implemented.
+/// Durable append-only store for MVCC commit records.
 pub trait VersionStore: Send + Sync {
-    /// Append a single block version record.
-    fn append_version(
-        &self,
-        cx: &Cx,
-        commit_seq: CommitSeq,
-        writer: TxnId,
-        block: BlockNumber,
-        bytes: &[u8],
-    ) -> Result<()>;
-
-    /// Append a commit marker record (atomicity boundary).
-    fn append_commit_marker(
-        &self,
-        cx: &Cx,
-        commit_seq: CommitSeq,
-        writer: TxnId,
-        write_count: u32,
-    ) -> Result<()>;
+    /// Append a single commit record (all writes for one commit_seq).
+    fn append_commit(&self, cx: &Cx, commit: &DurableCommitRecord) -> Result<()>;
 
-    /// Flush appended records to stable storage (fsync).
+    /// Flush appended records to stable storage (fsync/fdatasync).
     fn sync(&self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<()>;
 
-    /// Replay the log and invoke callbacks for committed versions.
-    fn replay(&self, cx: &Cx, apply: &mut dyn FnMut(BlockVersion)) -> Result<CommitSeq>;
+    /// Replay valid records in-order and apply committed versions.
+    fn replay(
+        &self,
+        cx: &Cx,
+        skip_up_to: CommitSeq,
+        apply: &mut dyn FnMut(&DurableCommitRecord),
+    ) -> Result<CommitSeq>;
 }
 ```
 
-The MVCC engine MAY bundle this behind a higher-level `MvccBlockManager` implementation; the key requirement is that the version store can be replayed deterministically.
+The MVCC engine MAY bundle this behind a higher-level `MvccBlockManager`; the hard requirement is deterministic replay with explicit corruption boundaries.
 
 #### 5.9.4 On-Disk Log Format (v1)
 
-The overlay file is a linear sequence of records:
-
-1. **Header** (single record, must be first)
-2. Repeated `{ VERSION* , COMMIT }` groups
-
-Each record is:
+The durable log is a linear byte stream:
 
-- A fixed-size header with a **magic**, a **type**, a **payload length**, and a **checksum**
-- Followed by the payload bytes
+1. **File header** (exactly once, first 16 bytes).
+2. Repeated **commit records**.
 
-Record header (conceptual):
+File header (`16` bytes):
 
 | Field | Size | Notes |
 |------|------|------|
-| magic | 4 B | `"FFSV"` |
-| kind | 1 B | `1=HEADER`, `2=VERSION`, `3=COMMIT` |
-| reserved | 3 B | must be 0 |
-| payload_len | 4 B | little-endian |
-| payload_crc32c | 4 B | CRC32C of payload bytes |
+| magic | 4 B | `0x4D56_4357` (`WAL_MAGIC`) |
+| version | 2 B | `1` (`WAL_VERSION`) |
+| checksum_type | 2 B | `0` = CRC32C |
+| reserved | 8 B | writer sets to zero |
 
-`HEADER` payload (must include enough to prevent accidental reuse against the wrong base device):
+Commit record framing:
 
-- `format_version: u32` (currently `1`)
-- `block_size: u32`
-- `base_fingerprint: [u8; 32]` (BLAKE3 of the base device identity; exact definition is implementation-chosen but MUST be stable)
-
-`VERSION` payload:
+| Field | Size | Notes |
+|------|------|------|
+| record_len | 4 B | LE length of bytes after this field |
+| record_type | 1 B | `1` = COMMIT |
+| commit_seq | 8 B | LE u64, strictly increasing in file order |
+| txn_id | 8 B | LE u64 writer transaction identifier |
+| num_writes | 4 B | LE u32 |
+| writes | variable | repeated write entries (below) |
+| record_crc32c | 4 B | CRC32C over `record_type..writes` (excludes `record_len` and CRC field) |
 
-- `commit_seq: u64`
-- `writer_txn: u64`
-- `block_number: u64`
-- `bytes_len: u32` (must equal `block_size`)
-- `bytes: [u8; bytes_len]`

-`COMMIT` payload:
+| Field | Size | Notes |

-- `commit_seq: u64`

+Normative ordering/dedup rules before serialization:
 
-**Checksum choice:** CRC32C is sufficient to detect torn/incomplete writes and random corruption. The durability layer MAY additionally store a BLAKE3 hash per block version for stronger integrity checks, but CRC32C is the required minimum.
+1. A transaction MUST serialize writes in ascending `block_number` order.
+2. At most one write per `block_number` may appear in one commit record.
+3. If a transaction stages the same block multiple times, only the last staged bytes are serialized.
+4. `commit_seq == u64::MAX` and `txn_id == u64::MAX` are reserved and invalid on replay.
 
 #### 5.9.5 Commit Protocol and Crash Consistency
 
-To make a commit crash-safe, the system MUST enforce:
+Crash-safe mode (`sync_on_commit=true`) MUST enforce:
 
-1. All `VERSION` records for a commit are durable before the `COMMIT` record is appended.
-2. The `COMMIT` record is durable before the commit is considered durable/visible after restart.
+1. In-memory FCW/SSI validation succeeds.
+2. Commit sequence allocation is monotonic and unique.
+3. The commit record append succeeds.
+4. The appended bytes are synced before returning success.
 
-Concrete protocol:
+Reference protocol:
 
 ```
 PROCEDURE durable_commit(T):
-  validate_fcw(T)                         // in-memory check (Phase A)
-  seq = allocate_commit_seq()             // monotonically increasing
-
-  // Deterministic ordering for reproducible logs.
-  writes = sort_by_block_number(T.staged_writes)
-
-  FOR EACH (block, bytes) in writes:
-    append VERSION(seq, T.txn_id, block, bytes)
-  sync()                                  // ensures all VERSION records are durable
-
-  append COMMIT(seq, T.txn_id, len(writes))
-  sync()                                  // ensures COMMIT marker is durable
-
-  publish_to_in_memory_index(seq, writes) // versions become visible immediately after commit returns
+  validate_fcw_ssi(T)
+  seq = allocate_commit_seq()
+  writes = canonicalize(T.staged_writes)      // dedup + block_number sort
+  rec = encode_commit(seq, T.txn_id, writes)  // includes CRC32C
+  append(rec)
+  sync()                                      // required in crash-safe mode
+  publish_to_in_memory_index(seq, writes)
   RETURN seq
 ```
 
-If the process crashes:
+Crash outcome matrix:
+
+| Crash Point | Replay Result |
+|-------------|---------------|
+| Before append starts | Commit absent |
+| Mid-record append (truncated tail) | Commit absent; tail discarded |
+| After full append but before sync (crash-safe mode) | Commit absent or present (depends on device flush behavior); never partially applied |
+| After sync returns success | Commit present |
 
-- After writing some `VERSION` records but before the `COMMIT` marker: the commit is treated as **absent** on replay.
-- After writing the `COMMIT` marker: the commit is treated as **present** and its versions are applied on replay.
+If `sync_on_commit=false`, the system is in throughput mode, not strict crash-safe mode.
 
 #### 5.9.6 Recovery Procedure (Replay)
 
-Recovery scans the overlay log sequentially:
+Replay algorithm:
 
-1. Validate `HEADER` (magic/version/block_size/base_fingerprint).
-2. Accumulate `VERSION` records for a given `commit_seq` into a temporary buffer.
-3. When a matching `COMMIT` record is encountered, apply the buffered versions in deterministic order.
-4. Stop at the first invalid record (bad magic, impossible length, checksum failure) and treat the remainder of the file as truncated tail.
+1. Validate file header (`magic`, `version`, `checksum_type`).
+2. Scan commit records sequentially from byte offset `HEADER_SIZE`.
+3. For each decodable commit record:
+   - reject and stop if `commit_seq` is non-monotonic (`<= last_replayed_seq`),
+   - reject and stop on reserved sequence/id values,
+   - optionally skip `commit_seq <= checkpoint_seq` when replaying after checkpoint load,
+   - apply writes atomically to in-memory MVCC structures.
+4. On first `NeedMore` (truncated record), checksum failure, malformed length/type, or monotonicity violation: stop replay and treat that record plus suffix as discarded tail.
+5. Truncate the durable stream to the last valid byte offset.
 
-This yields:
+Replay outputs:
 
-- `last_commit_seq` = highest committed `commit_seq` applied
-- `next_commit_seq` = `last_commit_seq + 1`
+- `last_commit_seq`: highest commit sequence successfully applied.
+- `next_commit_seq`: `last_commit_seq + 1`.
+- `records_discarded`: count of invalid/tail records ignored.
 
-**Replay MUST NOT publish versions for any `commit_seq` lacking a valid `COMMIT` marker.**
+Replay MUST be idempotent: re-running replay against unchanged bytes produces the same in-memory state.
 
 #### 5.9.7 GC / Watermarks / Compaction (strategy)
 
@@ -1855,13 +1852,53 @@ This yields:
 - `watermark = min(active snapshots).high`
 - per block, keep the **newest** version with `commit_seq <= watermark` (the keeper) plus all newer versions
 
-**Physical compaction (recommended):** the overlay file is append-only, so disk usage grows. When the overlay exceeds a size threshold, a background compactor MAY:
+**Physical compaction (recommended):** append-only logs grow. A compactor MAY:
+
+1. Take an MVCC barrier (exclusive) for a stable live-set at watermark.
+2. Emit a new log with only live versions and valid commit framing.
+3. `sync()` the new stream and atomically replace the old one.
+
+Compaction MUST preserve snapshot semantics (same GC-SAFE rule).
+
+#### 5.9.8 Compatibility / Versioning Rules
+
+1. `WAL_VERSION` controls incompatible wire changes.
+2. Reserved header bytes MUST be written as zero; readers MAY ignore non-zero values for forward compatibility diagnostics.
+3. Unknown `record_type` is a hard replay boundary (discard tail from that record onward).
+4. Any future checksum algorithm requires a versioned compatibility path (new `checksum_type` with explicit reader support).
+5. Schema evolution MUST preserve deterministic replay and explicit corruption boundaries.
+
+#### 5.9.9 Durable Invariants (bd-h6nz.1.1)
+
+| ID | Invariant | Enforcement Point |
+|----|-----------|-------------------|
+| **D1** | Commit sequence in durable stream is strictly increasing | replay scanner + commit allocator |
+| **D2** | A replayed commit is all-or-nothing (never partial writes) | record framing + CRC + atomic apply |
+| **D3** | Truncated/corrupt tail cannot mutate previously replayed state | replay stop-at-first-invalid rule |
+| **D4** | One block appears at most once in a commit record | canonicalize-before-encode |
+| **D5** | `commit()` success in crash-safe mode implies synced durable bytes | commit protocol (`sync_on_commit=true`) |
+| **D6** | Replay is deterministic for identical byte input | monotonic scan + deterministic apply order |
+| **D7** | `next_commit_seq` after replay is `last_commit_seq + 1` | replay finalization |
+| **D8** | Reserved sentinel IDs (`u64::MAX`) are never accepted | replay validation |
+
+#### 5.9.10 Validation Matrix and Logging Contract
+
+Stable scenario IDs for deterministic validation:
+
+| Scenario ID | Contract | Surface |
+|-------------|----------|---------|
+| `MVCC_DURABLE_WAL_001` | Round-trip commit encode/decode + replay determinism (`D1`, `D6`) | unit (`ffs-mvcc::wal` + `persist`) |
+| `MVCC_DURABLE_WAL_002` | Truncated tail ignored; prior commits preserved (`D2`, `D3`) | unit + e2e crash harness |
+| `MVCC_DURABLE_WAL_003` | Corrupt CRC discarded and tail truncated (`D2`, `D3`) | unit + e2e fault injection |
+| `MVCC_DURABLE_WAL_004` | Non-monotonic `commit_seq` rejected (`D1`) | unit replay tests |
+| `MVCC_DURABLE_WAL_005` | Commit success requires sync in crash-safe mode (`D5`) | unit with injected sync failures |
+| `MVCC_DURABLE_WAL_006` | Checkpoint + replay skip-up-to is deterministic (`D6`, `D7`) | integration/e2e |
 
-1. Take an MVCC barrier (exclusive) to obtain a stable view of which versions are live at the watermark.
-2. Write a new overlay file containing only live `VERSION` records (preserving original `commit_seq` values) plus `COMMIT` markers.
-3. `sync()` the new file, then atomically replace the old overlay via `rename()`.
